items.title: Ep20. Why did the Big Bang happen?
items.desc: Why did the Big Bang happen and was it really a giant explosion? In this mind-stretching episode, Lily and Max explore one of science’s biggest mysteries in a kid-friendly way. They discover how the universe began expanding, why scientists think space was once extremely hot and dense, what clues are left behind from the early universe, and why some questions are still being investigated today. It is a cosmic adventure full of galaxies, mystery boxes, star confetti, and very big “why?” questions.

items.title: Ep19. How does soap clean hands?
items.desc: In this episode of Little Ears, Big Ideas, Lily and Max explore how soap cleans hands. They discover why water alone is not always enough, how tiny soap molecules grab greasy dirt, and why rubbing, rinsing, and bubbles help wash germs away. It is a bubbly, squeaky, science-filled handwashing adventure for curious kids.

items.title: Special Episode. Why do kids always say they're not cold?
items.desc: In this funny Big Ears Edition, Lily and Max investigate why kids can stand in winter wearing shorts and a T-shirt while insisting they are “not cold.” From busy bodies and brave legs to jacket battles and emergency hoodies, this episode helps grownups understand what might really be going on, while giving kids a giggle too.

items.title: Ep18. Why can we see lightning, but it does not smash our backyard?
items.desc: Why can we see lightning flashing across the sky, but it doesn’t always hit our backyard? In this episode, Lily and Max explore what lightning really is, why it can look close when it is far away, why thunder comes after the flash, and how lightning finds its path through the air. With giant earmuffs, cloud batteries, zigzag zaps, and simple storm safety tips, this episode helps curious kids understand lightning in a fun, reassuring way.

items.title: Ep17. Why do different countries speak different languages?
items.desc: In this episode, Arthur asks why people in different countries speak different languages. Lily and Max take a fun world tour through mountains, accents, writing systems, travelling words, baby language detectives, and why words can carry family, culture, history and home. Along the way, they discover that languages are not just different sounds, they are also people’s stories.
